title: Small Teams vs. Big Teams: Productivity & Meeting Madness! #shorts
summary: A comparison of productivity and meeting overhead between small and large teams. The commentary argues that smaller agile teams accomplish tasks more efficiently while larger teams become bogged down in meetings and coordination overhead.
writeup: This short from Cyril Imhof contrasts small agile teams with large teams on productivity and efficiency. The argument: large teams lose time to excessive meetings and coordination overhead, while smaller teams operate more efficiently. The central takeaway is that efficiency tends to improve as team size shrinks.
